core_takeaway: Agent_1 finished ahead because the repeated anchor-first default preserved Quill and then attached Apple to Agent_1, yielding a stronger two-item bundle than Agent_3's Stone+Pencil. This looks like a qualified first-position advantage through agenda setting, not pure dominance, because Agent_3 also shaped and ultimately proposed the consensus allocation.
why_or_counterexample: Agent_1 used the first speaking slot to make Quill non-negotiable and later repeatedly framed Apple-to-Agent_1 as the default follow-up. Other agents adopted that frame, and by round 5 every formal proposal matched it. The counterexample is that Agent_3, the last speaker, introduced the eventual Quill/Jewel/Stone plus Apple/Pencil structure early and the accepted proposal was Proposal #3 by Agent_3; so the advantage is agenda-setting and default control, not unilateral extraction.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by prioritizing Quill and later repeatedly opened each round with the anchor lock plus Follow-up A. Agent_2 and Agent_3 largely echoed that anchor framing, while Agent_3 kept pressing Stone+Pencil as the payoff-preserving version.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private plans across agents converged on the same anchor logic: Quill to Agent_1, Jewel to Agent_2, Stone to Agent_3, with Apple/Pencil as the adjustable pair. Agent_1's private strategy consistently treated Apple-to-Agent_1 as the default and Pencil movement as conditional.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Early proposals were self-interested and failed. By round 5 all three proposals were identical: Agent_1 got Apple+Quill, Agent_2 got Jewel, and Agent_3 got Stone+Pencil; Proposal #3 was unanimously accepted.
phase_notes.private_voting: Early private votes rejected bundles that left an agent with too little or zero utility. Round 5 votes accepted the identical anchor proposal: Agent_1 cited Quill+Apple as its top two, Agent_2 cited preserved anchors and momentum, and Agent_3 cited Stone+Pencil.
phase_notes.reflection: Reflections repeatedly identify the anchors as the stable consensus mechanism and Apple/Pencil allocation as the blocker. They recommend keeping Follow-up A as default and using Follow-up B only with unanimous consent and offsets.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker set the Quill and Apple agenda
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 spoke first and immediately made Quill the central requirement while identifying Apple as the next valuable item. That early frame made Agent_1's top-plus-secondary bundle the reference point for later rounds.

evidence.quote: secure Quill, since it dominates my payoff

mechanisms.theme: Repeated defaults hardened into the final proposal
mechanisms.explanation: Across rounds, Agent_1 kept reopening with the same anchor lock and Follow-up A. By round 5, all agents submitted that same allocation, so Agent_1's early default became the consensus object.

evidence.quote: Follow-up A (default finish): Apple -> Agent_1, Pencil -> Agent_3
Follow-up A assigns Apple (0) to Agent_1 and Pencil (4) to Agent_3

mechanisms.theme: The swing-item framing favored Agent_1's second item
mechanisms.explanation: The final deal gave Agent_1 Quill+Apple, which Agent_1 privately described as its top two, while Agent_3 got Stone+Pencil. Since both are discounted in the same final round, the raw bundle difference explains the first-minus-last gap.

evidence.quote: gives Agent_1 a valuable second item
gives Agent_1 Quill + Apple (your top two)
This yields a strong payoff for Agent_3 (Stone+Pencil)

mechanisms.theme: Agent_3 meaningfully shaped the accepted outcome
mechanisms.explanation: The last-position agent was not merely dragged along. Agent_3 publicly articulated the final topology in round 1, protected Stone+Pencil throughout, and the system ultimately accepted Proposal #3 by Agent_3 unanimously.

mechanisms.theme: Self-serving first-agent attempts were constrained by unanimity
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 could not force maximal allocations simply by proposing first. Early bundles that overfavored Agent_1 or excluded Agent_3 failed; consensus arrived only after the first-position agenda was made compatible with the shared anchors.

evidence.quote: Proposal 1 leaves Agent_3 with no items
